name: Los Angeles, California
seo_title: Los Angeles, California Sales Tax Calculator
meta_description: The combined sales tax rate in Los Angeles, California is 9.75%. Enter a price to calculate the exact sales tax and total by product category.
intro_text: The combined sales tax rate in Los Angeles, California is 9.75%, made up of state, county, and local district rates. Enter a purchase amount below to see the exact tax for what you're buying.
seo_content:
How much is sales tax in Los Angeles, California?

The sales tax rate in Los Angeles is 9.75%. That combines California's statewide rate of 6.25% with 3.5% in local tax collected by Los Angeles County and any city or special districts covering the address. Local rates aren't the same across a metro area, so the total can change from one ZIP code to the next — the Los Angeles sales tax calculator above uses the local rate so your estimate matches what you'd actually pay at checkout.

Working out the tax on a purchase

To do it by hand, multiply the price by 9.75%. A $100.00 purchase in Los Angeles adds about $9.75, for a total of $109.75. The calculator handles this for any amount and breaks the result down by jurisdiction, so you can see how much goes to the state, to Los Angeles County, and to local districts. Enter a price and choose a category to get an exact figure for your purchase.

What's taxable, and what isn't

Not everything is taxed the same way. Most tangible goods are fully taxable, but groceries and prescription medicine are often exempt or taxed at a reduced rate, and some services fall outside sales tax altogether. Prepared food, clothing, and general merchandise are usually taxed in full. Switch the category in the calculator to see how California treats a specific purchase before you buy it or set up tax on what you sell.

Common questions

Why is the rate different in nearby cities? Counties, cities, and special districts each set their own local sales tax, and they don't all charge the same amount. A neighboring town can sit in a different district and land a fraction of a percent higher or lower, even when the state rate is identical.

How often does the Los Angeles rate change? State rates rarely move, but local rates can change when voters approve new district taxes, usually at the start of a calendar quarter. If you sell here, it's worth confirming the current figure before each filing — Zamp tracks those changes across every U.S. jurisdiction and files automatically.

name: Portland, Oregon
meta_description: Portland, Oregon has no general sales tax. Use the calculator to check tax by product category and see your total.
intro_text: Portland, Oregon doesn't charge a general sales tax. Use the calculator below to confirm how specific product categories are treated.
seo_content:
Does Portland, Oregon have sales tax?

Oregon is one of a small group of states with no statewide sales tax, so purchases in Portland generally aren't taxed at the register. A $100.00 item costs $100.00 — nothing is added at checkout, and no local sales tax applies in Portland either, which is why the calculator above returns a rate of 0%.

What this means for shoppers

The price on the shelf is usually the price you pay. You won't see a separate sales tax line on most receipts here, which is one reason Oregon draws shoppers making larger purchases. Other taxes can still apply in specific cases — excise taxes on goods like fuel or tobacco, or lodging taxes on hotel stays — but those are separate from general sales tax.

Selling from Portland

Because Oregon has no general sales tax, most sellers based in Portland don't collect one on local sales. If you ship to customers in other states, you may still have sales tax obligations there based on where those customers are. Zamp helps sellers work out exactly where they need to register and collect, and handles the filing in each state.
